mappings.rationale: Directly covers Islam including its history, beliefs, practices, and institutions — the primary subject of section 828.
Directly covers the Quran, the subject of 828.B.1 on its form, contents, views about its origin, and interpretations.
Covers the life and teachings of the Prophet Muhammad, directly relevant to 828.A.2 on the origin of Islam.
Directly covers the history of Islam from its origins through the modern period, the subject of 828.A.
Directly covers Islamic law (Shariah), the subject of 828.B.3 and 828.C.3.d on law and jurisprudence.
Covers Islam's worldwide development and contemporary dimensions, relevant to 828.A.6-7 on Islam in the modern world and Islam today.
Directly covers Islamic philosophy, the subject of 828.B.4 on Islamic theology and philosophy.
Avicenna is one of the major Islamic philosophers, relevant to 828.B.4 on the major schools of Islamic philosophy.
Covers Islamic fundamentalism, relevant to 828.A.6 on Islam in the modern world and 828.C.2 on forms of Islam.
Covers Islam in comparative perspective with Judaism and Christianity, relevant to understanding Islam's place among the Abrahamic traditions.
Covers theological approaches relevant to Islamic theology and doctrines concerning God described in 828.C.1.a.
Covers concepts of God including the Islamic understanding of monotheism, relevant to 828.C.1.a on doctrines concerning God.
Covers mystical traditions relevant to understanding Sufism, the mystical path in Islam described in 828.B.5.
Covers pilgrimage including the hajj to Mecca, one of the Five Pillars listed in 828.C.3.a.
Covers Iran as a major Shia Muslim country, relevant to 828.C.2.d on Shiism and its subsects.
The Crusades were a major encounter between Islam and Christianity, relevant to 828.A.5 on the Middle Ages of Islam.
The Israeli-Palestinian conflict has significant Islamic religious dimensions, relevant to 828.A.6-7 on Islam in the modern world.
The Silk Road facilitated the spread of Islam across Central Asia, relevant to 828.A.3-4 on the early expansion and development of Islam.
Covers interpretive theory relevant to Quranic interpretation and Islamic hermeneutics, relevant to 828.B.1 on interpretations of the Quran.
Provides comparative context for understanding Islam within the broader study of religion.
Covers the relationship between Islam and warfare including concepts of jihad, relevant to 828.A and the military expansion of early Islam.
The Bible is referenced in Islamic tradition as earlier scripture, relevant to 828.C.1.e on doctrines concerning the nature of prophecy.
Covers the spread of Islam in Africa, relevant to 828.A.3-4 on the expansion of Islam beyond Arabia.
Covers Southeast Asia where Islam is a major religion, relevant to 828.A.3-4 on the expansion of Islam.
Covers Pakistan as a major Islamic nation, relevant to 828.A.7 on Islam today.
Covers concepts of blasphemy in Islam including the anti-iconic principle, relevant to 828.C.4.c on the effect of the anti-iconic principle.
Covers philosophical questions about God relevant to Islamic theological discourse in 828.B.4.
Covers debates about secularism in Islamic societies, relevant to 828.A.6 on Islam in the modern world.
Sikhism emerged partly from Islamic influences, relevant to understanding the context of Islam in South Asia discussed in 828.A.
